memoryArtifacts.headline: Rebecca's ancestors
memoryArtifacts.description:
A long time ago, in a land faraway, the groundwork was being established for Reformation of the Gospel, this is the story of two of our ancestors who helped.

zh.lifeSummary: Alice Ann Sands是John Sands的女兒，於1502年出生在英國貝德福爾斯特亞特利。她於1534年與Edmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterbury在英國英格蘭貝德福爾盧頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1544年之後在英國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Alice Ann Sands是John Sands的女儿，于1502年出生在英国貝德福爾斯特亞特利。她于1534年与Edmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterbury在英国英格兰貝德福爾盧頓结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1544年之后在英国。

nameMeanings.name: Sands
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name from Middle English sandes ‘sands’, for someone who lived at or near a sandy place, or a habitational name from a place so named, such as Sands in Carlisle (Cumberland) or Sands Cottages in West Hoathly (Sussex). See Sand , of which Sands may sometimes be a plural form or a variant, with excrescent -s. Compare also Sandes and Sandys .

History: Comfort Sands, a revolutionary patriot born in 1748 at what is now Sands’ Point, Long Island, NY, was descended from James Sand(y)s (1622–95), who emigrated from Reading, Berkshire, England, to Plymouth, MA, and followed Anne Hutchinson to Westchester County, NY, and subsequently RI. In 1661 he settled on Block Island, RI.

variants.name: Sand
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Bertel, Ejnar, Lars, Levinus, Nels, Niels, Thor.

English, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived on or near a patch of sandy soil, from the vocabulary word sand (German Sand). As a Swedish name it was often purely ornamental, and as a Jewish artificial. It is also found in France (see 3 below).
English: habitational name from a place such as Send (Surrey) or Sound (Cheshire), named with Middle English sand, sond ‘sand’.

variants.name: Sandes
meanings: Galician: habitational name from any of various places so named in Galicia (Spain). Compare   Sandez  .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

en.lifeSummary: Alice Ann Sands was born in 1502, in Streatley, Bedfordshire, England as the daughter of John Sands. She married Edmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterbury in 1534, in Luton, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died after 1544, in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Alice Ann Sands est née en 1502, à Streatley, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, fille de John Sands. Elle a épousé Edmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterbury en 1534, à Luton, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée après 1544, à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Alice Ann Sands wurde 1502 in Streatley, Bedfordshire, England, als Tochter von John Sands geboren. Sie hat 1534 in Luton, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom Edmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterbury geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb nach 1544 in England.

it.lifeSummary: Alice Ann Sands è nata il nel 1502 nel Streatley, Bedfordshire, England come figlia di John Sands. Lei sposato Edmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterbury nel 1534, nel Luton, Bedfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì dopo il 1544, nel England.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice Ann Sandsは1502年にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアストレアトリーでJohn Sandsの娘として生まれた。1534年にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアルトンでEdmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterburyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1544年以後にイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice Ann Sands(은)는 1502년 영국 베드퍼드실르 스트리틀리에서 John Sands의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1534년 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 루턴에서 Edmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterbury(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1544년 이후 영국에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Alice Ann Sands nasceu em 1502, em Streatley, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra filha de John Sands. Ela casou-se com Edmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterbury em 1534, em Luton, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu após 1544, em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Alice Ann Sands родилась в 1502 г. в местности стритли, бедфордшир, Англия. Её отца звали John Sands. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Edmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterbury в 1534 г. в местности лутон, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла после 1544 г. в местности Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Alice Ann Sands nació en 1502, en Streatley, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra como hija de John Sands. Se casó con Edmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterbury en 1534, en Luton, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió después de 1544, en Inglaterra.
